diagrams.description: Microeconomics examines how individual households and firms make decisions to allocate scarce resources. Its core objective is to understand consumer utility maximization and producer profit maximization. These behavioral principles are studied within different market structures — perfect competition, monopoly, and oligopoly — and lead to outcomes that are evaluated in terms of efficiency, equity, and potential market failures. When markets fail, government intervention may be justified. Together, these three pillars — behavior, market structures, and welfare — lead to the optimal allocation of scarce resources.
